articles.title: LEAKER SAYS THERE'S NO BACK TO BLACK FOR THE IPHONE 18 PRO
articles.summary: Apple dropped the black color option for the iPhone 17 Pro. It now offers only blue, orange, and silver. Despite some user disappointment, the bold Cosmic Orange proved highly popular, especially in China, contributing to strong sales. A reliable leaker now suggests the black finish still won't return for the iPhone 18 Pro, indicating Apple is sticking with more distinctive, status-driven color choices.

articles.title: CURSOR REFRESHES ITS VIBE CODING PLATFORM WITH FOCUS ON AI AGENTS
articles.summary: Cursor released Cursor 3, a new version of its AI coding platform that focuses on AI agents to help developers automate programming tasks. The platform features a chatbot interface where developers can describe features in natural language and choose which AI model to use, with both cloud-based and desktop agents working together. New features include Design Mode for UI editing, multi-agent task completion, and improved code review processes.

articles.title: BUILD WITH MORE CONTEXT AND MORE CONTROL IN FIGMA MAKE
articles.summary: Figma Make now supports Make kits and Make attachments, two features designed to bring real design system and project context into AI-generated prototypes. Make kits allow teams to package their component libraries and usage guidelines so prototypes start from production-aligned code rather than generic components. Make attachments let designers supply actual files — such as PDFs, datasets, and screenshots — so prototypes reflect real constraints and content instead of idealized approximations.

articles.title: HOW TO CREATE DIGITAL CONCEPT ART USING BLENDER AND PHOTOSHOP
articles.summary: A concept artist created The Vault to overcome creative overwhelm by building a consistent world where new ideas could be easily integrated, resulting in a detailed scene of an underground control room separating humanity from creatures below. The workflow combines pre-built assets, Blender scene construction, thumbnailing, and Photoshop paintovers, with an emphasis on storytelling details, visual hierarchy, and efficient techniques like kitbashing and “clown passes.”

articles.title: THE MANY PATHS IN THE LIFE OF A DESIGNER
articles.summary: There is no single “correct” career path—advice that claims otherwise is usually personal projection, especially in a rapidly changing world shaped by AI and shifting job markets. Instead, careers are nonlinear and shaped by individual choices, environments, and life events, so the best path comes from understanding your own strengths, motivations, and circumstances while staying adaptable and persistent.

articles.title: HUMAN-CENTRED DESIGN HAS GROWN UP. IT'S TIME WE DID TOO
articles.summary: Early UX pioneers like Steve Krug and Don Norman championed usability and frictionless design, but this success helped create today's attention economy, where seamless experiences often enable manipulation, data extraction, and reduced user autonomy. A new era—driven in part by EU regulations—redefines design as protecting human rights and societal wellbeing, shifting the goal from helping users complete tasks to ensuring systems respect, inform, and preserve human freedom.

articles.title: AI COWORKER
articles.summary: Viktor is the AI coworker that connects to 3,000+ tools and does the work.

articles.title: DESIGN SYSTEMS REPORT 2026
articles.summary: A survey with 147 practitioners revealed that design systems are transitioning from peak expectations into a "trough of disillusionment" where teams struggle with understaffing, reduced executive buy-in (dropping from 42% to 32%), and persistent adoption challenges.

articles.title: PENTAGRAM REVEALS CODE-BREAKING TITLE SEQUENCE FOR RIZ AHMED'S BAIT
articles.summary: Pentagram's Luke Powell and Jody Hudson-Powell created a technically sophisticated yet emotionally meaningful title design for Bait, a comedy-drama by Riz Ahmed, using a color-filter typography system that reveals multiple meanings of the word “bait” to reflect shifting identity and perception. The design extends across the show's visuals, blending espionage aesthetics with deeper cultural and linguistic layers, and marks another milestone in the duo's influential, concept-driven career.

articles.title: SAN MIGUEL'S NEW LOOK MADE USE OF EYE TRACKING TECHNOLOGY
articles.summary: San Miguel has unveiled its first redesign in a decade, using consumer research and neuroscience tools like EEG and eye-tracking to refine packaging for stronger shelf visibility, premium perception, and emotional impact. The updated look—subtle but more polished—is rolling out across Europe alongside its “Spanish Summer, No Matter When” campaign, reinforcing its association with sunny, social moments.

articles.title: AI'S AESTHETICS OF FAILURE
articles.summary: AI technology, despite its sophistication, has been plagued by poor aesthetics that even supporters like Nvidia's CEO call "slop." OpenAI shut down its video generation app Sora this week after usage plummeted from 6 million to 1.5 million monthly downloads due to users finding it unpleasant and unfun to use. The platform was dominated by tasteless content and glitched-out videos that failed to engage audiences beyond initial novelty.

articles.title: DESIGNBOOM'S ULTIMATE GUIDE TO MILAN DESIGN WEEK 2026
articles.summary: Milan Design Week 2026, which runs from April 20–26, will transform the city into a sprawling showcase of design, architecture, and innovation.

articles.title: TEN MUST-KNOW MODERN JEWELLERY DESIGNERS
articles.summary: Visionary jewelry designers from London to Lahore are creating modern pieces that draw inspiration from their local landscapes, translating street signs, folkloric symbols, and everyday objects into elaborate designs.

articles.title: SIX DESIGNS THAT REIMAGINE HOW WE INTERACT WITH SOFTWARE
articles.summary: Product teams at ServiceNow, Ticketmaster, and Affirm are using Figma Make to build conviction faster through prototyping.
